message.content:
{
  "materials": {
    "ppt": {
      "deckMode": "teacher-delivery",
      "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
      "templateName": "Kamalaniketan Session PPT Template",
      "themeId": "kamalaniketan-modern",
      "title": "Integrated Science: Assessment & Revision",
      "presentationTitle": "Science Review: Concepts, Data & Processes",
      "presentationGoal": "To consolidate key scientific concepts through visual analysis, formula application, and process review in preparation for assessment.",
      "audience": "Middle School Integrated Science Students (Grades 6-8)",
      "theme": "kamalaniketan-modern",
      "themeTokens": {
        "fonts": {
          "heading": "Outfit",
          "body": "Inter"
        },
        "colors": {
          "primary": "#36ADAA",
          "secondary": "#1EABDA",
          "accent": "#DE8431",
          "background": "#EEF4F7",
          "surface": "#FFFFFF",
          "text": "#0F172A",
          "mutedText": "#64748B"
        },
        "visualStyle": {
          "topBarStyle": "bold color band",
          "cardStyle": "rounded modern educator cards",
          "visualFrameStyle": "large clean visual panel"
        }
      },
      "assetSearchPlan": {
        "preferredSources": [
          "Internal SVG",
          "Google Imagen API"
        ],
        "safeSearch": true,
        "licensingNotes": [
          "Use internally generated images and in-app SVG diagrams only."
        ],
        "fallbackStrategy": "Prefer SVG diagrams for concept/process slides and the Google Imagen API for all picture-based visuals."
      },
      "licenseChecklist": [
        "Review generated visuals for classroom accuracy before final export.",
        "Ensure scientific symbols in formulas are rendered correctly."
      ],
      "presentationWarnings": [
        "Do not include untaught future-session content.",
        "Focus on revision of previously taught concepts only."
      ],
      "coverageSummary": {
        "learningOutcomesCovered": [
          "LO1: Recall and apply scientific formulas",
          "LO2: Interpret data from graphs and diagrams",
          "LO3: Explain key scientific processes"
        ],
        "topicsCovered": [
          "Scientific Formulas & Equations",
          "Data Analysis & Graphs",
          "Process Flow Review"
        ],
        "taughtConceptsCovered": [
          "Formula application in context",
          "Reading X and Y axes correctly",
          "Step-by-step process sequencing"
        ],
        "omittedContent": []
      },
      "slides": [
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "title_identity",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Science Review: Concepts & Data",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 1,
          "slideType": "hook | concept | comparison | process | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Integrated Science: Assessment & Revision",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Session Overview"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Introduce the session as a comprehensive review and assessment preparation.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students understand today is for consolidating knowledge through visuals and practice.",
          "layout": "Title + Hero Visual",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Integrated Science Review Session",
            "Focus: Formulas, Data & Processes",
            "Goal: Assessment Readiness"
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Science Review: Concepts, Data & Processes",
            "Session 12 of Integrated Science Unit",
            "Ready for your assessment?"
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Welcome students to the review session.",
            "Explain that today we will revisit key concepts through visuals and problem-solving rather than just reading text.",
            "Emphasize that this is a safe space to practice before the formal assessment.",
            "Transition: Let's look at what we need to know."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": "A dynamic collage showing a beaker, a graph line rising, and a formula equation floating in a clean educational style.",
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "Hero visual combining elements of chemistry (beaker), physics/math (graph/line), and general science to represent the integrated nature.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Visual representation of Integrated Science themes",
              "searchQuery": "science laboratory beaker graph formula educational illustration flat design",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Illustration of science tools including a beaker, graph chart, and mathematical symbols.",
              "placementHint": "Right panel full bleed"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Session Identity",
            "type": "concept map",
            "instructions": [
              "Display session title prominently."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Fade in the hero visual slowly.",
            "Animate text bullet points one by one."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 2
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "learning_outcomes",
          "templateSlideTitle": "What We Will Achieve Today",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 2,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Learning Outcomes & Success Criteria",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1",
            "LO2",
            "LO3"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Session Goals"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Set clear expectations for what students will be able to do by the end of the session.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students know exactly how they will demonstrate their understanding today.",
          "layout": "3-Column Outcome Cards",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Apply formulas correctly in problems",
            "Interpret graphs and data accurately",
            "Explain scientific processes step-by-step"
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "1. Apply Formulas: Solve numericals using correct units.",
            "2. Analyze Data: Read axes, trends, and draw conclusions.",
            "3. Explain Processes: Sequence steps logically in diagrams."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Read through the three main outcomes clearly.",
            "Outcome 1 is about precision with numbers and symbols (Physics/Chemistry focus).",
            "Outcome 2 focuses on visual literacy—reading what a graph tells us beyond just looking at it.",
            "Outcome 3 connects to Biology or Earth Science processes where sequencing matters.",
            "Ask students: Which of these feels most challenging? Note responses."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "Three distinct cards side-by-side, each with an icon representing the outcome (Calculator for formulas, Eye/Graph for data, Flowchart arrow for processes).",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Iconography for outcomes",
              "searchQuery": "calculator icon graph chart flowchart arrow icons set educational style",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Set of three icons: calculator, graph chart, and flow arrow.",
              "placementHint": "Top left of each card"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Outcomes Map",
            "type": "comparison chart",
            "instructions": [
              "Visualize the three distinct skills."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Reveal each outcome card sequentially as discussed.",
            "Highlight keywords in bold color on hover or click."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 3
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "prerequisite_knowledge",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Recall: What We Know Already",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 3,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Prerequisites & Key Recall",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Prior Knowledge Activation"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Activate prior knowledge to bridge into the revision content.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students recall essential formulas and definitions needed for today's tasks.",
          "layout": "Title + Recall Grid",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Recall: Basic Units (m, s, kg)",
            "Remember: Standard Formulas (v=d/t)",
            "Review: Graph Axes Labels"
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Units of Measurement: Length (m), Time (s), Mass (kg).",
            "Key Formula: Speed = Distance / Time.",
            "Graph Basics: X-axis is Independent, Y-axis is Dependent."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Quick fire round: Ask students to shout out the unit for speed.",
            "Write 'Speed' on board and ask a student to write the formula next to it.",
            "Clarify the difference between independent (X) and dependent (Y) variables with a quick example.",
            "Transition: Now that we have our tools ready, let's look at how they work in practice."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A 'Cheat Sheet' style grid showing common units and the speed formula with a small diagram of axes.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Visual aid for recall",
              "searchQuery": "science unit symbols meter second kilogram educational infographic",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Infographic showing standard SI units for length, time, and mass.",
              "placementHint": "Left panel"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Recall Grid",
            "type": "comparison chart",
            "instructions": [
              "Display common symbols."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Fade in the cheat sheet grid.",
            "Animate the formula components appearing one by one."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 3
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "lesson_hook",
          "templateSlideTitle": "The Mystery of the Missing Data",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 4,
          "slideType": "hook | concept | comparison | process | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Hook: Can You Solve This?",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO2"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Data Interpretation Challenge"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Capture attention with a real-world problem that requires data analysis.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students are engaged in solving a mystery using scientific reasoning.",
          "layout": "Title + Mystery Image",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "A graph shows strange behavior.",
            "Why did the temperature drop?",
            "What does this mean for our experiment?"
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Scenario: A plant's growth rate dropped suddenly in Week 4.",
            "Question: What environmental factor could cause this?",
            "Task: Analyze the data to find the answer."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Present a scenario where experimental results don't match expectations.",
            "Show a graph with a sudden dip in growth rate (or similar metric).",
            "Ask students to hypothesize causes based on prior knowledge of factors affecting plants (light, water, soil).",
            "This hooks them into the importance of accurate data interpretation for LO2.",
            "Transition: Let's look at how we analyze such graphs systematically."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A line graph showing a plant growth curve with a sharp dip highlighted in red.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Hook visual",
              "searchQuery": "line graph sudden drop educational illustration mystery science",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Graph showing a line with a sharp downward spike.",
              "placementHint": "Center of slide"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Mystery Graph",
            "type": "flowchart",
            "instructions": [
              "Show the dip clearly."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Animate the line drawing to trace the path, then pause at the drop.",
            "Flash the red highlight on the anomaly."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 4
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "topic_introduction",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Core Concepts: Formulas & Data",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 5,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Topic Introduction: The Toolkit",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1",
            "LO2"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Scientific Formulas & Data Types"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Formally introduce the core concepts that will be revisited in depth.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students understand the relationship between formulas, variables, and data representation.",
          "layout": "Title + 2-Column Text",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Scientific Formulas define relationships.",
            "Variables: Independent vs. Dependent.",
            "Data Types: Qualitative & Quantitative."
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Formulas are mathematical sentences describing nature.",
            "Independent Variable (X): What we change.",
            "Dependent Variable (Y): What we measure.",
            "Qualitative Data: Descriptive observations.",
            "Quantitative Data: Numerical measurements."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Define a formula as more than just math; it's a rule of nature.",
            "Reiterate the X/Y axis distinction clearly, linking back to the hook graph.",
            "Distinguish between qualitative (what you see) and quantitative (numbers), crucial for lab reports.",
            "Transition: Now let's look at how we visualize these relationships."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A split diagram showing a formula on the left and variable definitions on the right.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Concept definition",
              "searchQuery": "scientific variables independent dependent educational infographic",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Diagram explaining Independent and Dependent variables.",
              "placementHint": "Right panel"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Concept Definitions",
            "type": "comparison chart",
            "instructions": [
              "Compare qualitative vs quantitative."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Fade in definitions one by one.",
            "Highlight 'Independent' and 'Dependent' keywords."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 4
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "core_concept_a",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Deep Dive: Scientific Formulas",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 6,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Core Concept A: Formula Application",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Formula Derivation & Use"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Build deep understanding of how to manipulate and apply formulas correctly.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students can identify variables, substitute values, and calculate results with units.",
          "layout": "Title + Formula Card",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Identify the correct formula for the problem.",
            "List knowns (given) and unknowns (to find).",
            "Substitute values carefully.",
            "Check units before calculating."
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Step 1: Identify Formula (e.g., Speed = d/t)",
            "Step 2: List Knowns & Unknowns",
            "Step 3: Substitute Values",
            "Step 4: Calculate with Units"
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Walk through the four-step process for solving any numerical problem.",
            "Emphasize that skipping Step 2 (listing knowns) is a common error source.",
            "Stress unit consistency—e.g., converting km to m if necessary before calculating.",
            "Transition: Let's see this in action with a worked example."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A step-by-step flowchart showing the formula application process.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Process visualization",
              "searchQuery": "step by step problem solving science math educational diagram",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Flowchart showing the 4 steps of formula application.",
              "placementHint": "Center"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Formula Application Steps",
            "type": "flowchart",
            "instructions": [
              "Show the logical flow from identification to calculation."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Animate each step appearing sequentially.",
            "Highlight 'Units' in green when mentioned."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 5
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "core_concept_b_visual",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Deep Dive: Data Interpretation",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 7,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Core Concept B: Reading Graphs & Diagrams",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO2"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Graph Interpretation"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Teach students how to extract meaning from visual data representations.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students can identify trends, slopes, and anomalies in scientific graphs.",
          "layout": "Title + Large Diagram",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Read the Title first for context.",
            "Check Axis Labels and Units.",
            "Identify Trends (Positive/Negative/No change).",
            "Spot Anomalies or Outliers."
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "1. Read Title: What is being measured?",
            "2. Check Axes: X = Independent, Y = Dependent.",
            "3. Analyze Trend: Steep slope? Flat line?",
            "4. Note Anomalies: Points that don't fit."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Explain the 'Title First' rule—context is key to interpretation.",
            "Discuss how a steep slope indicates rapid change, while a flat line means no change over time.",
            "Point out what an outlier might indicate (experimental error or new discovery).",
            "Transition: Now let's apply this reading skill to a real example."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A detailed line graph with annotations pointing out title, axes labels, trend lines, and an outlier.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Graph analysis example",
              "searchQuery": "labeled scientific line graph educational diagram anomaly highlighted",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Labeled graph showing axes, title, trend lines, and an outlier point.",
              "placementHint": "Full bleed background"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Graph Analysis Guide",
            "type": "labelled diagram",
            "instructions": [
              "Show how to read a graph systematically."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Animate arrows pointing to each feature (Title, Axes, Trend) as discussed.",
            "Highlight the outlier in red briefly then fade it back or keep distinct."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 5
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "worked_example",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Worked Example: Solving a Problem",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 8,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Example: Calculating Speed from Data",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Numerical Problem Solving"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Model expert thinking by solving a problem step-by-step.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students see exactly how to apply the formula and handle units correctly.",
          "layout": "Title + Step-by-Step Solution",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Problem: Car travels 150km in 2.5 hours.",
            "Formula: Speed = Distance / Time.",
            "Calculation: 150 / 2.5 = 60 km/h."
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Given: d = 150 km, t = 2.5 h",
            "Formula: v = d/t",
            "Substitute: v = 150 / 2.5",
            "Result: v = 60 km/h"
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Read the problem aloud.",
            "Ask students to identify knowns and unknowns before writing anything down.",
            "Write the formula on board, then substitute numbers together with class.",
            "Perform calculation step-by-step, emphasizing that 150 divided by 2.5 is not just '6' but '60'.",
            "Stress adding units (km/h) to the final answer.",
            "Transition: Now you try a similar problem."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A clean layout showing the problem statement on top and the solution steps below with clear arrows.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Worked example visual",
              "searchQuery": "math word problem car speed calculation educational diagram",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Diagram showing a car and the steps to calculate its speed.",
              "placementHint": "Left panel"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Speed Calculation Steps",
            "type": "flowchart",
            "instructions": [
              "Show substitution and calculation."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Reveal each step of the solution one by one.",
            "Animate the final answer appearing with a 'check' mark or highlight."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 5
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "guided_practice",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Guided Practice: Your Turn to Solve",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 9,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Practice Activity: Analyze & Calculate",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1",
            "LO2"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Student Practice"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Students actively apply the concepts to new problems.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students practice solving numericals and interpreting graphs independently or in pairs.",
          "layout": "Title + Activity Instructions",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Task 1: Calculate speed for a train (200km, 4h).",
            "Task 2: Interpret the provided growth graph.",
            "Discuss your answers with a partner."
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "Activity: Work in pairs.",
            "Problem A: Find speed of Train X (d=200km, t=4h).",
            "Graph B: What happened to the plant at Week 3?",
            "Time: 10 minutes."
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Distribute worksheets or project problems on screen.",
            "Instruct students to work in pairs (Think-Pair-Share).",
            "Circulate room, checking for unit errors and axis misinterpretations.",
            "Ask one pair to share their answer for Task A.",
            "Ask another pair to explain the trend they saw in Graph B.",
            "Transition: Let's check our understanding with a quick quiz."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "A slide showing two distinct problems side-by-side for the students to solve.",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Practice problem visual",
              "searchQuery": "train speed calculation graph plant growth educational worksheet style",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Visual representation of the practice problems.",
              "placementHint": "Center"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Practice Problems",
            "type": "comparison chart",
            "instructions": [
              "Display two distinct tasks."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Fade in the problems.",
            "Count down timer animation for 10 minutes (optional)."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 8
        },
        {
          "templateId": "kamalaniketan-session-12",
          "templateSlideKey": "quick_assessment",
          "templateSlideTitle": "Quick Assessment: Check Your Understanding",
          "isOptionalSlotFilled": true,
          "slideNumber": 10,
          "slideType": "concept | summary",
          "slideTitle": "Assessment: Do You Get It?",
          "learningOutcomeIds": [
            "LO1",
            "LO2"
          ],
          "topicCoverage": [
            "Formative Assessment"
          ],
          "teacherIntent": "Evaluate student understanding of the session's key points.",
          "studentTakeaway": "Students self-assess their grasp of formulas and data interpretation.",
          "layout": "Title + Questions",
          "bulletPoints": [
            "Q1: What is the unit for speed?",
            "Q2: Which axis shows time in a distance-time graph?",
            "Q3: If slope increases, what happens to speed?"
          ],
          "onSlideText": [
            "1. Unit of Speed? (m/s or km/h)",
            "2. Time on X-axis or Y-axis?",
            "3. Steeper slope = Faster/Slower?"
          ],
          "speakerNotes": [
            "Ask students to hold up fingers for answers (1, 2, or 3) or use mini-whiteboards.",
            "Q1: Check if they know it's distance/time units.",
            "Q2: Reinforce X-axis = Time/Independent variable.",
            "Q3: Connect slope steepness directly to speed magnitude.",
            "Review answers immediately and clarify misconceptions."
          ],
          "visualAttribution": {
            "visualPlan": null,
            "svgDiagram": null
          },
          "visualPlan": "Three clear question cards with space for student response indicators (e.g., icons).",
          "assets": [
            {
              "purpose": "Assessment questions",
              "searchQuery": "quiz questions science speed graph educational infographic",
              "sourceSite": "Google Imagen API",
              "sourceUrl": "",
              "licenseType": "Internally generated or Creative Commons",
              "attributionText": "",
              "altText": "Three quiz questions displayed clearly.",
              "placementHint": "Center"
            }
          ],
          "svgDiagram": {
            "title": "Quick Quiz",
            "type": "comparison chart",
            "instructions": [
              "Display the three assessment questions."
            ],
            "svgCode": ""
          },
          "animationHints": [
            "Reveal each question one by one.",
            "Show correct answer after student response (fade in green text)."
          ],
          "timeEstimateMinutes": 5
